title: ClickUp vs Monday.com
description: Comprehensive ClickUp vs Monday.com comparison for 2026. We analyze features, pricing, integrations, security, performance, and user experience across 35+ criteria. ClickUp wins with a 4.4/5 rating. Includes detailed feature tables, pricing comparison, decision matrix, AI capabilities, migration advice, and expert buying recommendations for startups, SMBs, and enterprises evaluating project management solutions.

verdict: After evaluating both platforms across dozens of criteria including feature completeness, pricing value, security posture, integration ecosystem, and real-world user satisfaction, we name ClickUp as the overall winner in this head-to-head comparison. ClickUp achieves a 4.4/5 rating compared to Monday.com's 4.4/5. This 0.0-point gap reflects ClickUp's advantages in core functionality, ecosystem maturity, and overall value proposition. However, this does not mean Monday.com is without merit. For teams with specific requirements such as , niche workflow needs, or existing ecosystem investments, Monday.com remains a compelling option. Our verdict is based on hands-on testing, analysis of ClickUp user reviews across multiple platforms, and evaluation against our standardized criteria framework. For a detailed breakdown of our methodology, see our software evaluation checklist. For a complete ranking of all project management tools, visit our best project management list. New to the category? Start with our project management buying guide. Understanding the pricing models of ClickUp and Monday.com is critical for analysis. ClickUp is priced at Freemium (Free–$19/user/mo) with a freemium with per-user premium model. Monday.com costs Paid ($10–$20/seat/mo) using a per-user monthly subscription approach. Both platforms offer tiered pricing structures designed to accommodate different organization sizes and use cases. Entry-level plans provide essential functionality for small teams and startups, while enterprise tiers unlock advanced features like , , and dedicated support. When evaluating total cost, consider not just subscription fees but also implementation costs, training requirements, and ongoing administration. Our TCO calculator can help estimate the full financial impact of each platform. Integration capabilities often determine whether a platform fits into your existing technology stack. ClickUp integrates with leading tools in the project management ecosystem, including Slack, Google Drive, GitHub, GitLab, and more. Monday.com connects with Slack, Microsoft Teams, Google Drive, Jira, among others. Both platforms offer RESTful APIs and support for custom integrations. Native integrations reduce the need for middleware like Zapier vs Make for common workflows. For teams using tools like , , or , both platforms offer pre-built connectors. However, the depth and reliability of these integrations can vary significantly between the two platforms. Security is a top concern when choosing project management software, especially for enterprises handling sensitive data. ClickUp holds SOC 2 Type II, GDPR certifications and complies with GDPR, CCPA, SOC 2. Monday.com is certified with SOC 2 Type II, SOC 3, ISO 27001 and follows GDPR, CCPA, HIPAA. Both platforms provide at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3), granular integration, and detailed . For healthcare organizations, compliance availability may be a deciding factor. Similarly, firms should verify Type II reports before committing. Our security software buying guide provides deeper insights into evaluating platform security for your organization. Performance and reliability directly impact team productivity. ClickUp delivers consistent performance with 99.9% uptime SLA for project management workloads. Monday.com maintains strong performance metrics with comparable availability guarantees. Both platforms invest heavily in infrastructure reliability. SLA guarantees, historical uptime data, and industry benchmarks should factor into your evaluation. Enterprise deployments typically require 99.95% or higher uptime commitments. Artificial intelligence capabilities are increasingly differentiating project management platforms. ClickUp offers AI features including AI project manager, AI writing assistant, Smart search. Monday.com provides monday AI, AI workflow builder, AI status updates, Smart formula generation. Both platforms leverage large language models and to enhance core functionality. Our AI adoption report shows that project management tools with integrated AI capabilities see significantly higher user satisfaction scores. AI software statistics indicate that organizations using AI-enhanced tools report 30-40% productivity improvements in core workflows. API quality and developer experience are critical factors for technical teams. ClickUp offers a comprehensive API for custom integrations and workflow automation. Monday.com provides robust API access with extensive documentation. Both platforms support and for event-driven integrations. Developer experience, rate limiting, and documentation quality should be evaluated if your team plans extensive custom development. Customer support quality can make or break your experience with any project management platform. ClickUp maintains a support rating of 4.1/5 and releases updates bi-weekly. Monday.com scores 4.2/5 for support with weekly update cycles. Both platforms offer standard support channels including , community forums, and ticket-based support. Premium tiers typically include response times, dedicated account management, and priority support. The right choice between ClickUp and Monday.com often depends on your specific use case and organization profile. For startups and small teams evaluating project management solutions, are paramount. ClickUp offers a generous free tier to get started, while Monday.com provides value-focused plans. Enterprise organizations with complex requirements should evaluate including advanced , , and dedicated support. Financial services and organizations will need to verify and compliance before making a decision. Construction and manufacturing teams have unique workflow requirements that may favor one platform over the other. Similarly, marketing agencies and retail businesses will prioritize different feature sets. Our industry adoption research shows how different sectors choose between leading platforms. For hospitality and education sectors, TCO analysis and timelines often determine platform selection. We recommend evaluating both tools against your specific operational workflows before committing to a multi-year contract. Enterprise organizations evaluating ClickUp vs Monday.com need to look beyond feature checklists. Single sign-on, , , and options are critical requirements for most large organizations. ClickUp provides enterprise-grade capabilities including , certification, and dedicated infrastructure options. Monday.com offers comparable enterprise features with its own set of and security frameworks. Enterprise buyers should review each platform's , , and before signing contracts. Our enterprise SaaS pricing research provides benchmarks for negotiating contracts. Startups need project management tools that balance affordability with growth capacity. ClickUp's pricing starts at Freemium (Free–$19/user/mo), making it accessible for early-stage companies. Monday.com at Paid ($10–$20/seat/mo) is priced for growing businesses. Key considerations for startups include , for custom development, and the ability to if you need to switch platforms later. Our startup software guide covers these topics in depth. Migrating between project management platforms requires careful planning to avoid data loss and workflow disruption. Whether you are moving from ClickUp to Monday.com or vice versa, understanding the migration complexity is essential. ClickUp migration is rated as Moderate complexity, while Monday.com migration is Medium. Key considerations include data export compatibility, API-based migration tools, and endpoint mapping between the two platforms. Best practices for migration include: - Conducting a thorough before migration - Running parallel systems during transition - Training team members on the new platform - Validating data integrity post-migration - Setting up and access controls in the target environment Our implementation best practices guide provides a detailed migration framework for project management platform transitions. To simplify your ClickUp vs Monday.com decision, use this decision matrix based on common organizational profiles: Choose ClickUp if: - You need the most comprehensive feature set in the project management category - Your team values All-in-one productivity replacing multiple tools - and are primary decision factors - You need proven reliability and support - Integration with existing tools is a high priority Choose Monday.com if: - Your organization has specific niche requirements that Monday.com addresses better - Monday.com's Visual work platform that adapts to any team's workflow with customizable boards and automations aligns with your team's workflow - constraints make Monday.com's pricing model more attractive - Your team is already invested in the Monday.com ecosystem - You need specific that only Monday.com offers SaaS buying decisions should never be made lightly. After researching hundreds of project management platforms and analyzing user reviews, here is our expert buying advice for choosing between ClickUp and Monday.com: 1. Define your requirements first. Use our framework to document must-have features versus nice-to-have capabilities. 2. Set a realistic budget. Consider not just subscription costs but implementation, training, and ongoing administration expenses. 3. Test both platforms. Most project management vendors offer free trials or demos. Use this time to validate critical workflows. 4. Check integrations. Verify that your essential tools integrate natively with each platform. 5. Read recent reviews. Our ClickUp review and Monday.com review pages have detailed user feedback and expert analysis. Common mistakes buyers make: - Focusing only on price without considering - Skipping for regulated industries - Overlooking limitations that affect custom integrations - Ignoring and support quality - Choosing without structured evaluation criteria - Failing to involve end users in the evaluation process - Underestimating migration complexity and data transfer requirements For organizations with complex requirements, our software evaluation service provides personalized recommendations based on your specific needs.
